title: Nav Header
description: A horizontal navigation header that spans the top of the application. Contains the logo, app switcher (bento), primary navigation, and user actions.
whenToUse: At the top of every authenticated page in the app shell. To house the brand mark, app switcher, and primary global actions.
whenNotToUse: On marketing or landing pages (use a simpler header). On mobile-only views, consider a collapsible pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Inner content uses a 3-column grid (left, center, right) constrained to max-width 1440px.
Bento trigger exposes aria-expanded and aria-haspopup; activating it toggles the dropdown.
Bento dropdown closes on Escape (whether focus is on the trigger or inside the panel) and on outside click; on Escape, focus returns to the trigger.
Icon-only ghost buttons in the account pill have aria-label.
My Work badge announces meaning (e.g. "N unread") to screen readers, not just the bare number.
When showSearch=false the centered search field is omitted and the layout remains balanced.
When showMyWork=false the My Work pill is omitted from the right region.
When titleOnly=true the title-area renders as a bare paragraph/xl-bold page title with no bento trigger chrome (no icon, no chevron, no pill background, no shadow).
Inside the bento dropdown, sections (tiles / list / footer) are separated by `udc-divider-horizontal` rows with data-padding="sm" to match Figma's `_udc-nav-header_dropdown` anatomy.

props.name: showSearch
props.description: When true, renders the centered search field. Mirrors the Figma `Show Search Box` boolean on the udc-nav-header component set.

props.name: showMyWork
props.description: When true, renders the My Work pill (with notification badge) in the right region. Mirrors the Figma `Show My Work` boolean on the udc-nav-header component set.

props.name: titleOnly
props.description: When true, the title-area renders as a bare page title (paragraph/xl-bold, no leading icon, no chevron, transparent pill chrome, radius 8). Use when the page does not need an app switcher. Markup: apply `data-title-only="true"` to `.udc-nav-title-area`. Mirrors the Figma `Title Only=True` variant on the `_udc-nav-header_title-area` set (5586:8859).

events.name: bento-toggle
events.description: Fires when the bento (app switcher) dropdown opens or closes. The trigger button toggles aria-expanded; the wrapper toggles data-open.

events.name: bento-dismiss
events.description: Fires when the bento dropdown closes due to Escape key or outside click.

slots.name: brand
slots.description: Logo / brand mark on the far left. Figma binds this to surface/brand-bold.

slots.name: title-area
slots.description: Page-title pill that doubles as the app-switcher trigger. Wraps icon + page title + chevron and expands the bento dropdown. CSS: `.udc-nav-title-area`. Figma source: `_udc-nav-header_title-area` (5586:8858).

slots.name: center
slots.description: Centered region — by default a search field. Hidden when showSearch=false.

slots.name: actions
slots.description: Right-side primary actions. By default the My Work pill; hidden when showMyWork=false.

slots.name: account
slots.description: Right-side account-actions pill containing three icon-only ghost buttons.

slots.name: bento.tiles
slots.description: Row of nav tiles inside the bento dropdown (top of the panel). CSS: `.udc-nav-bento__tiles`.

slots.name: bento.list
slots.description: Main list of destinations inside the bento dropdown. CSS: `.udc-nav-bento__list`. Built on udc-list.

slots.name: bento.footer
slots.description: Footer row inside the bento dropdown (e.g. "Customize menu"). CSS: `.udc-nav-bento__footer`.

states.name: default
states.description: Bento dropdown closed; data-open absent on the wrapper; aria-expanded='false' on the trigger.

states.name: bento-open
states.description: Bento dropdown visible; data-open='true' on the wrapper; aria-expanded='true' on the trigger.

states.name: title-only
states.description: Title-area renders as a bare paragraph/xl-bold page title with no bento chrome. Toggled via `data-title-only="true"` on `.udc-nav-title-area`. Mirrors Figma `Title Only=True` (5586:8859).

states.name: not-applicable
states.description: Figma does not define hover / focus / disabled states at the header level. Those states are inherited from nested components (search, button-ghost, badge, list).

keyboard.action: Move focus through nav header items
Close the bento dropdown when open

screenReader.announcement: Outer container exposes role="banner" so the header is identified as a landmark.
Trigger exposes aria-haspopup and aria-expanded; expanded state is announced as the dropdown opens or closes.

screenReader.trigger: Focus moves to an icon-only button
screenReader.announcement: Each icon-only ghost button in the account pill exposes an aria-label describing its action.
Badge count is exposed via accessible name (e.g. "My Work, 5 unread") rather than a bare number.

wcag.howMet: Semantic <header> + landmark role; bento trigger is a real <button>; account actions are real buttons.
Text and meaningful icon contrast meets 4.5:1 (text) / 3:1 (non-text). Currently unverified — see knownIssues for divergences between Figma intent and CSS.
All interactive elements are keyboard-reachable in DOM order.
Visible focus indicators on all focusable elements (bento trigger + sub-components).
Bento trigger exposes name/role/state; icon-only ghost buttons require aria-label.

knownIssues: Authoring errors ("Component set for node has existing errors") originate from the udc-badge component set (5794:7948) on the badge Figma page. They surface during deep inspection of nav-header because the My Work pill nests a udc-badge instance. The canonical udc-nav-header set (5684:11340) itself reads cleanly. The 🔴 blocked status on the nav-header page persists until that upstream Figma issue is resolved on the badge page.

Logo glyph in examples/default.html is an `apartment` Material Symbol; Figma's canonical logo is a hand-drawn brand-mark vector in surface/brand-bold (#023674). Replicating the exact vector requires an SVG export from Figma, and the `surface/brand-bold` color is in a Figma collection that is not currently imported into the doc-site's semantic tokens. The doc-site falls back to a Material Symbol with --uds-color-icon-interactive until both gaps are closed.

Frame 31 max-width (1440) is bound in Figma to `content-container/max-width-nav-container` — a Figma collection that is NOT currently in the imported token collections. CSS bakes 1440 as a literal until the collection is added to the token import scope.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Use <header role="banner"> as the outer element. The bento button should have aria-expanded and aria-haspopup.
